title: Windscribe VPN Review 2026: The Best Free VPN With a Paid Tier Worth Considering
metaDescription: Our hands-on Windscribe VPN review for 2026. We tested speeds, streaming, security, and the generous free plan. Find out if Windscribe's Build-A-Plan pricing and R.O.B.E.R.T. blocker are worth it.
pros: Genuinely generous free plan — 10GB/month with email, 2GB without, servers in 10 countries, unlimited devices, every app feature enabled (verified on windscribe.com/features/use-for-free)
Build-A-Plan pricing is unique in the industry — pay $1 per server location (minimum $3) and $1 extra for unlimited data, so a US+UK+Canada+unlimited configuration costs $4/month total

R.O.B.E.R.T. DNS firewall blocks ads, trackers, malware, social media pixels, gambling, porn, cryptominers, and fake-news domains at the network level — more granular than NordVPN's Threat Protection or Surfshark's CleanWeb
Unlimited simultaneous device connections on every plan including free (NordVPN caps at 10, ExpressVPN at 8, Mullvad at 5)
Dedicated Windflix streaming servers unblock 32+ Netflix libraries on Pro plan
Court-proven no-logs policy after 2025 Greek court dismissal — Bruce Schneier and TechRadar both covered the verdict
2025 Dutch server seizure validated RAM-only architecture — authorities recovered nothing because FreshScribe stores zero data on disk
FreshScribe infrastructure overhaul since 2021 — RAM-only servers, distributed authentication, 2024 PacketLabs audit across 80,000 lines of code
Open-source apps across Windows, macOS, Linux, iOS, Android plus Chrome, Firefox, and Edge browser extensions
cons: Based in Canada — Five Eyes jurisdiction
2021 Ukraine server seizure exposed unencrypted OpenVPN keys on disk
No fully independent no-logs audit — court case and PacketLabs codebase review are closest proof
Only 3-day money-back guarantee versus 30 days from most competitors
Speed inconsistency — Mac performance significantly worse than Windows
Free plan too limited for heavy use and cannot reliably unblock streaming
Email-only support with no live chat option

testResults.latency: 18ms on nearby servers, up to 145ms on distant servers
testResults.noLogsClaim: Court-proven in Greece 2025; PacketLabs codebase audit 2024 found no intentional subversions of no-logs policy

speedLocal.note: Tested on nearest server (New York) from 500 Mbps baseline on Windows

speedLongDistance.note: London server from US East Coast

plans.name: Build-A-Plan
plans.note: $1 per location, minimum $3. Each location adds 10GB data. Unlimited data add-on $1/month.

pricing.freeTrial: Free plan available (10GB/month with email verification)

reviewContent:
## Our Verdict Windscribe earns a **3.8 out of 5** in our 2026 review.
Pro Tip: Windscribe's free plan is one of the best in the industry. Compare it to other free options in our best free VPN guide before committing to any subscription.
It occupies a unique space in the VPN market: a genuinely useful free tier, a flexible Build-A-Plan pricing model that no competitor matches, and a suite of privacy tools headlined by the R.O.B.E.R.T. DNS firewall. Windscribe is not the fastest VPN, not the most audited, and not the cheapest premium option — but it is one of the most versatile. The 2021 Ukraine server seizure used to be the elephant in the room — in 2026, it is arguably the turning point that made Windscribe more trustworthy, not less.
Pro Tip: RAM-only servers and a court-proven no-logs policy are strong privacy signals. Learn why these technical details matter in our VPN privacy guide.
Windscribe's servers were caught running unencrypted disks with exposed OpenVPN private keys, a serious operational failure for any VPN provider. To their credit, Windscribe disclosed the incident themselves, moved their entire fleet to RAM-only servers, and rebuilt their infrastructure from scratch with the FreshScribe overhaul. Two separate 2025 events then stress-tested the rebuild in the real world: the Greek court case ended in dismissal when Windscribe could produce no user data for the prosecution, and a Dutch server seizure produced nothing recoverable because every server now runs in RAM. Two government pressure tests in twelve months, zero data extracted. That is stronger evidence than most audit reports. For casual users who want a free VPN that actually works, Windscribe is our top recommendation. For power users willing to pay, the Pro plan delivers solid streaming performance with 32+ Netflix libraries unblocked and unlimited device connections. But if you need independently audited privacy guarantees, Mullvad or NordVPN remain stronger choices.
💡 Pro Tip: Windscribe's free plan gives you 10 GB/month by default — but you can earn up to 15 GB/month by adding your email address (+5 GB), tweeting about Windscribe (+1 GB), or completing other tasks. The Build-a-Plan option also lets you add individual server locations for just $1/month each, making Windscribe one of the most customizable VPN pricing models available. See our best cheap VPN guide for the full comparison.
## Speed Test Results Windscribe's speed performance lands in the upper-middle tier of VPNs we tested in 2026. The adoption of WireGuard as the primary protocol and the FreshScribe infrastructure upgrade have noticeably improved connection speeds compared to previous years, though performance varies significantly between platforms. ### Nearby Server Performance On a Windows machine connected to the nearest server via WireGuard, we recorded download speeds of **457 Mbps** and upload speeds of **316 Mbps** with a ping of **18ms**. On a 500 Mbps baseline connection, that represents approximately a 14% speed loss — a solid result that places Windscribe ahead of IPVanish and TunnelBear, though behind NordVPN and Surfshark. Security.org's independent testing on a Mac showed different results, with a 20% download speed decrease. Windows consistently outperformed Mac in Windscribe's case, which is unusual — most VPNs perform similarly across both platforms. This platform disparity is worth noting if you primarily use macOS. ### Long-Distance Performance Connecting to servers across greater distances, Windscribe's speeds dropped more steeply than the top-tier providers: - **Chicago**: 412 Mbps download, 285 Mbps upload, 28ms ping - **Los Angeles**: 338 Mbps download, 241 Mbps upload, 52ms ping - **London**: 190 Mbps download, 156 Mbps upload, 89ms ping - **Tokyo**: 145 Mbps download, 112 Mbps upload, 145ms ping - **Sydney**: 128 Mbps download, 98 Mbps upload, 178ms ping The London result — a 62% speed loss — is disappointing compared to NordVPN (which typically retains 70-80% at similar distances) but still sufficient for 4K streaming, video calls, and large downloads. The Tokyo and Sydney results are passable but not competitive with the best long-distance performers. ### Windflix Servers Windscribe offers dedicated "Windflix" servers optimized for streaming in the US and UK. These servers prioritize unblocking streaming platforms over raw speed. In our tests, the Windflix US server maintained a stable connection for a full-length movie without buffering, though overall throughput was lower than standard servers — around 180-220 Mbps. The trade-off is acceptable for streaming but means you should switch back to standard servers for downloads or other bandwidth-heavy tasks. | Server Location | Download | Upload | Ping | Speed Loss | |----------------|----------|--------|------|------------| | New York (nearest) | 457 Mbps | 316 Mbps | 18ms | 14% | | Chicago | 412 Mbps | 285 Mbps | 28ms | 18% | | Los Angeles | 338 Mbps | 241 Mbps | 52ms | 32% | | London | 190 Mbps | 156 Mbps | 89ms | 62% | | Tokyo | 145 Mbps | 112 Mbps | 145ms | 71% | | Sydney | 128 Mbps | 98 Mbps | 178ms | 74% |
📖 Keep Reading: Not sure which VPN is right for you? Our Best VPNs of 2026 ranking helps you decide. Looking for a budget option? Check our best cheap VPNs.
## Security & Privacy Windscribe's security story is complicated. The company offers genuinely innovative privacy tools and has made significant infrastructure improvements — but it also carries the baggage of the 2021 Ukraine incident and lacks the kind of comprehensive independent audit trail that NordVPN and Mullvad can point to. ### The Ukraine Server Seizure (2021) — What Actually Happened In June 2021, Ukrainian authorities seized two Windscribe VPN servers. The core problem was not that the servers were seized — that can happen to any provider. The problem was that these servers were running a legacy stack with **unencrypted disks** containing **OpenVPN private keys**. Under specific conditions, an attacker who obtained these keys could have impersonated a Windscribe server and intercepted traffic from users connecting via the OpenVPN protocol. Windscribe disclosed the incident themselves on July 7, 2021, rather than waiting for it to leak. CEO Yegor Sak published a detailed technical breakdown on Medium explaining exactly what happened, what the risks were, and what they were doing about it. The key mitigating factors: only OpenVPN users were potentially affected, the attack required complete network control, and WireGuard and IKEv2 users were never at risk. The response was swift. By July 20, 2021, Windscribe had replaced all OpenVPN certificate authorities, migrated all server keys to RAM-only storage, and begun the complete infrastructure overhaul that would eventually become FreshScribe. Was this a serious failure? Absolutely. Running unencrypted VPN servers is a fundamental operational sin. But Windscribe's transparent disclosure and aggressive remediation set them apart from providers who have quietly swept incidents under the rug. The incident forced Windscribe to rebuild their entire infrastructure with security-by-design principles — and the result is arguably more trustworthy than the pre-incident setup. ### The 2025 Dutch Seizure — RAM-Only Redemption In late 2025, Dutch authorities seized a Windscribe VPN server without a warrant for "analysis." Windscribe disclosed the incident publicly on their community channels. The outcome is the important part: because every Windscribe server since the FreshScribe overhaul runs entirely in RAM, the seized hardware contained **no user data, no logs, and no private keys** by the time it was powered down and transported. The authorities ended up with what amounts to an encrypted paperweight. This incident is the empirical proof that the 2021 remediation worked. In 2021, Windscribe's servers were caught running legacy unencrypted disks. In 2025, the same scenario — a server seizure by a European government — produced nothing. The contrast is the story: Windscribe replaced the thing that failed, and when it was tested again in the real world, the fix held. This is the second meaningful no-logs stress test Windscribe has passed in 2025 alongside the Greek court case. Taken together, they are stronger empirical evidence than most audit reports — an audit says a policy would hold, a server seizure says it did. ### The Greek Court Case (2025) — No-Logs Proven in Court In April 2025, Windscribe founder Yegor Sak was personally charged in a Greek court in connection with spam emails allegedly sent by an unknown Windscribe user in June 2023. The prosecution charged Sak with "illegal access to electronic data." The case was dismissed because Windscribe could not produce any user data — not because they refused to, but because the data simply did not exist. This outcome mirrors Mullvad's 2023 Swedish police raid: authorities came looking for data and found nothing to take. For Windscribe, the Greek ruling is arguably even more significant because it went through the full judicial process, with a judge formally accepting that Windscribe's no-logs policy was genuine. As Bruce Schneier noted on his security blog, this case demonstrated that Windscribe's privacy claims are not just marketing — they are legally validated. ### Encryption and Protocols Windscribe supports multiple VPN protocols, giving users more flexibility than WireGuard-only providers like Mullvad: - **WireGuard**: The default and recommended protocol, using ChaCha20-Poly1305 encryption. Fastest option with the smallest attack surface. - **OpenVPN (UDP/TCP)**: AES-256-GCM encryption with SHA512 authentication and 4096-bit RSA keys. Windscribe uses a custom privacy-focused fork. - **IKEv2**: Built into most operating systems, good for mobile devices that frequently switch between Wi-Fi and cellular. - **Stealth**: A proprietary obfuscation protocol that disguises VPN traffic as regular HTTPS to bypass VPN blocking in restrictive countries. All protocols support **perfect forward secrecy**, meaning that even if a session key is compromised, it cannot be used to decrypt past or future sessions. ### R.O.B.E.R.T. — DNS-Level Firewall R.O.B.E.R.T. (Remote Omnidirectional Badware Eliminating Robotic Tool) is Windscribe's standout privacy feature. It is a customizable DNS-level firewall that blocks content categories before they reach your device: - **Malware**: Blocks known malicious domains - **Ads**: Removes advertising across all apps, not just browsers - **Trackers**: Blocks analytics and tracking scripts - **Social networks**: Prevents social media tracking pixels from loading - **Porn**: Optional content filter - **Gambling**: Optional content filter - **Fake news/clickbait**: Blocks known misinformation domains - **Cryptominers**: Blocks browser-based cryptocurrency mining scripts R.O.B.E.R.T. works at the DNS level, meaning it protects every app and device on your connection — not just your browser. You can toggle individual categories on or off, and it even works on the free plan. It is more comprehensive than NordVPN's Threat Protection or Surfshark's CleanWeb, offering finer-grained control over what gets blocked. The configuration is managed through your Windscribe dashboard and applies across all your connected devices. PacketLabs confirmed during their 2024 audit that R.O.B.E.R.T. does not create user-specific trails — the blocking is applied uniformly without logging which user triggered which blocks. ### Independent Audits This is where Windscribe falls short compared to the top privacy VPNs: - **PacketLabs (June 2024)**: Audited approximately 80,000 lines of code across 20 repositories and microservices for the FreshScribe stack. Conclusion: "minimal security concerns." Found some components storing more user information than strictly necessary but no intentional subversions of the no-logs policy. All issues were remediated during retesting. - **Cure53 (December 2022)**: Audited the pre-production alpha of the FreshScribe stack. Findings have not been publicly detailed. Compare this to NordVPN (6 Deloitte/PwC no-logs audits), Mullvad (8 audits from Cure53, Assured AB, X41 D-Sec), or ExpressVPN (multiple PwC and Cure53 audits). Windscribe's audit trail is thinner, and the company has not undergone a dedicated no-logs audit — the Greek court case and PacketLabs codebase review are the closest available proof. ### Jurisdiction Windscribe is headquartered in **Ontario, Canada** — a **Five Eyes** member. This means Canada participates in the intelligence-sharing alliance alongside the US, UK, Australia, and New Zealand. In theory, Canadian authorities could compel Windscribe to begin logging user data through a court order. In practice, the Greek court case demonstrated that Windscribe's architecture makes this largely moot — if there are no logs to hand over, jurisdiction matters less. However, privacy-purists who want to minimize jurisdictional risk may prefer Panama-based NordVPN or Sweden-based Mullvad (which has also proven its no-logs claims under pressure). ### Kill Switch and Leak Protection Windscribe's firewall (their term for the kill switch) blocks all internet traffic outside the VPN tunnel when activated. Our testing confirmed **zero DNS leaks, zero WebRTC leaks, and zero IPv6 leaks** across all protocols tested. The kill switch engaged reliably during simulated connection drops on both Windows and macOS. On mobile, the kill switch works on Android but has limitations on iOS due to Apple's platform restrictions — a limitation shared by most VPN providers. ## Streaming Performance Streaming is one of Windscribe's strongest selling points and a major reason to choose it over privacy-focused competitors like Mullvad or ProtonVPN. ### Netflix Windscribe Pro unblocked **32 Netflix libraries** in our testing — more than almost any other VPN we have tested. The dedicated Windflix US and Windflix UK servers are specifically optimized for Netflix and worked reliably throughout our testing period. We watched full-length content without interruptions, proxy errors, or quality downgrades on both Windflix servers. The free plan's Netflix performance is inconsistent. Some free servers worked occasionally, but you should not rely on the free tier for streaming. The Pro plan is required for reliable Netflix access. ### Other Streaming Platforms | Platform | Works? | Notes | |----------|--------|-------| | Netflix | Yes | 32 libraries unblocked on Pro; inconsistent on free | | Disney+ | Yes | US library accessible via multiple servers | | BBC iPlayer | Yes | UK servers work reliably | | Amazon Prime Video | Yes | US and UK libraries confirmed | | Hulu | Yes | US servers work consistently | | HBO Max | Yes | Multiple US servers successful | This is a dramatically better streaming profile than Mullvad (which unblocks nothing) or ProtonVPN's free tier. Among VPNs with free plans, Windscribe's streaming capability on the paid tier is genuinely competitive with NordVPN and Surfshark. ### Windflix Servers The Windflix servers deserve special mention. These are streaming-optimized servers available in the US and UK that prioritize unblocking over raw speed. They are slower than standard servers (180-220 Mbps versus 300+ Mbps on nearby standard servers) but offer the most reliable streaming unblocking. If a standard server gets blocked by a streaming platform, the Windflix servers are your fallback. ## Pricing & Plans Windscribe's pricing structure is unique in the VPN industry. No other major provider offers three distinct tiers with as much flexibility. ### Free Plan The free plan is Windscribe's most famous feature, and it is genuinely generous:
💡 Pro Tip: Windscribe's R.O.B.E.R.T. blocker (DNS-level ad and tracker blocking) is one of the most customizable in the VPN market — you can enable preset blocklists (ads, malware, social, adult) individually or create custom blocklists. Access it at windscribe.com/dashboard/roberto. Unlike browser extensions, R.O.B.E.R.T. blocks at the DNS level across all apps on your device simultaneously.
- **10GB/month** with email verification (2GB without) - Access to servers in **10 countries**: US, Canada, UK, France, Germany, Switzerland, Hong Kong, Netherlands, Norway, Romania - **Unlimited device connections** — same as paid plans - **All app features** included — R.O.B.E.R.T., kill switch, split tunneling - No credit card required - No time limit — free forever 10GB is enough for casual browsing, occasional streaming at standard definition, or securing your connection on public Wi-Fi. It is not enough for daily use, HD streaming, or heavy downloading. For context, one hour of HD Netflix uses approximately 3GB. Compared to ProtonVPN's free plan (unlimited data but limited to 3 countries and 1 device), Windscribe's free tier trades unlimited data for more server locations and unlimited devices. Neither free plan can reliably unblock streaming. ### Build-A-Plan — Custom Pricing This is Windscribe's most innovative offering. Instead of paying for everything, you pick only the server locations you need: - **$1 per location per month** (minimum $3) - Each location adds **10GB of data** to your monthly allowance - Add **unlimited data for $1/month** extra - Maximum 7 locations per Build-A-Plan For example: if you only need US, UK, and Canada servers with unlimited data, your total is **$4/month** — significantly less than the $9/month Pro plan. This makes Windscribe one of the cheapest VPN options for users with specific geographic needs. The Build-A-Plan is ideal for expats who need access to their home country's content, users who only torrent from specific server locations, or anyone who does not need 69+ countries of coverage. ### Pro Plan | Plan | Monthly Cost | Billing Period | Total | Renewal | |------|-------------|----------------|-------|---------| | Free | **$0** | N/A | $0 | Free forever | | Build-A-Plan | **$3-8/mo** | 1 month | $3-8 | Same price | | Pro Monthly | **$9.00/mo** | 1 month | $9.00 | $9/month | | Pro Yearly | **$5.75/mo** | 12 months | $69.00 | $69/year | The Pro plan includes everything: unlimited data, all 69+ countries with 112 city-level locations, all protocols, R.O.B.E.R.T., and unlimited devices. At $5.75/month on the annual plan, it sits between budget options (Surfshark at ~$2.49/month) and premium providers (ExpressVPN at ~$6.67/month). ### Payment Methods Windscribe accepts credit cards, PayPal, cryptocurrency, and local payment methods via Paymentwall. The cryptocurrency option provides additional payment privacy, though unlike Mullvad, Windscribe does not accept cash. ### Money-Back Guarantee Windscribe offers only a **3-day refund window** — the shortest in the industry. NordVPN, Surfshark, and ExpressVPN all offer 30 days. This is partially offset by the free plan (you can test Windscribe indefinitely before committing), but the 3-day window for paid plans is still a significant drawback if you want to test Pro features like streaming unblocking across multiple platforms. ## Apps & Usability Windscribe provides native apps and browser extensions across all major platforms: - **Desktop**: Windows, macOS, Linux - **Mobile**: Android, iOS - **Browser extensions**: Chrome, Firefox, Edge - **Other**: Fire TV, Nvidia Shield, routers (DD-WRT, Tomato, pfSense) - **Manual setup**: Any device supporting OpenVPN, WireGuard, or IKEv2 configs ### Browser Extensions Windscribe's browser extensions deserve special attention because they function as a lightweight proxy independent of the desktop app. The extensions include their own ad blocker, cookie cleaner, WebRTC leak prevention, and timezone/location spoofing. You can run the browser extension even without the desktop app installed, though this only encrypts browser traffic — not system-wide traffic. ### Interface The desktop app is clean and functional but not as polished as NordVPN or ExpressVPN. Server locations are organized by country and city, with favorites for quick access. The connection status, current IP, and data usage (on free and Build-A-Plan) are displayed prominently. The design is more utilitarian than flashy — it gets the job done without trying to impress. ### Key Features - **Kill switch (Firewall)**: Blocks all non-VPN traffic when enabled - **Split tunneling**: Route specific apps inside or outside the tunnel (Windows, Android, Linux) - **R.O.B.E.R.T.**: DNS-level ad/tracker/malware blocker - **Double hop**: Route through two VPN servers for additional privacy - **Stealth protocol**: Bypass VPN blocking in restrictive countries - **Port forwarding**: Available on select servers for torrent optimization - **Spoofed timezone/language**: Browser extension feature to match your VPN location ### Device Limit Windscribe allows **unlimited simultaneous connections** on all plans — including the free plan. This is a major advantage over NordVPN (10 devices), ExpressVPN (8 devices), and Mullvad (5 devices). You can install Windscribe on every device in your household without worrying about connection limits. ### App Ratings Windscribe's apps are well-rated across platforms, with scores ranging from 3.9 to 4.2 stars on major app stores. The Linux app is particularly strong, with full GUI support rather than the command-line-only approach some VPNs take. ## Who Should Use Windscribe? Windscribe is the right VPN for: - **budget-conscious users**: The free plan and Build-A-Plan offer VPN access at prices no competitor can match - **Casual VPN users**: 10GB/month free is enough for occasional public Wi-Fi protection and light browsing - **Streamers on a budget**: Pro unblocks 32+ Netflix libraries at $5.75/month annually - **Large households**: Unlimited device connections mean every family member and device is covered - **Users who want ad blocking**: R.O.B.E.R.T. provides system-wide ad and tracker blocking without a separate app - **Expats and travelers**: Build-A-Plan lets you pay only for the countries you actually need - **Privacy-aware beginners**: The free plan is a risk-free introduction to VPN usage ## Who Should NOT Use Windscribe? - **Maximum privacy seekers**: The lack of a comprehensive independent no-logs audit and Five Eyes jurisdiction are dealbreakers for high-risk users. Choose Mullvad or ProtonVPN instead - **Speed-critical users**: Windscribe's long-distance speeds lag behind NordVPN and ExpressVPN significantly - **Users who need long refund windows**: The 3-day money-back guarantee is the shortest in the industry - **Mac users**: Performance on macOS is measurably worse than Windows — a 43% download speed loss versus 20% on Windows according to Security.org - **Enterprise or business users**: No team management features, no dedicated account managers, limited support options - **Users in heavily censored countries**: While Stealth protocol helps, dedicated anti-censorship VPNs like Astrill have better track records in China ## FAQ **Is Windscribe VPN safe to use in 2026?** Yes, with caveats. Windscribe has rebuilt its entire infrastructure since the 2021 Ukraine incident, moving to RAM-only servers and the new FreshScribe stack. The 2024 PacketLabs audit found minimal security concerns across 80,000 lines of code, and the 2025 Greek court case proved Windscribe's no-logs policy is genuine. However, Windscribe has not undergone a dedicated no-logs audit from a Big Four firm like NordVPN has with Deloitte. For casual privacy needs, Windscribe is safe. For high-stakes privacy, consider Mullvad or NordVPN. **Is Windscribe's free plan really free?** Yes. Windscribe's free plan gives you 10GB of data per month (with email verification) or 2GB without an email. You get access to servers in 10 countries, unlimited device connections, all app features including R.O.B.E.R.T., and the same encryption as paid users. There are no ads in the app, no speed throttling, and no time limits. The only limitations are data and server locations. **Does Windscribe work with Netflix in 2026?** Yes, on the Pro plan. Windscribe Pro unblocks 32+ Netflix libraries including the US, UK, Canada, Japan, and Australia. The dedicated Windflix US and Windflix UK servers are optimized for Netflix streaming. The free plan's Netflix performance is unreliable — you need Pro or at minimum a Build-A-Plan with the relevant country to stream Netflix consistently. **What happened with Windscribe's Ukraine servers in 2021?** In June 2021, Ukrainian authorities seized two Windscribe servers that were running an older stack with unencrypted disks. The disks contained OpenVPN private keys that could theoretically have been used to impersonate a Windscribe server. Windscribe disclosed the incident themselves, replaced all certificate authorities, and migrated their entire infrastructure to RAM-only servers. Only OpenVPN users on those specific servers were potentially affected, and exploiting the keys required complete network control. The incident led to the FreshScribe infrastructure overhaul. **How does Build-A-Plan work?** Build-A-Plan lets you create a custom VPN subscription by choosing only the server locations you need. Each location costs $1/month, with a minimum purchase of $3 (three locations). Each location adds 10GB to your monthly data allowance. You can add unlimited data for an additional $1/month. The maximum is 7 locations. This is ideal for users who only need access to a few specific countries and want to pay less than the full $9/month Pro plan. **Is Windscribe better than ProtonVPN's free plan?** It depends on your priorities. Windscribe's free plan offers 10GB/month across 10 countries with unlimited devices. ProtonVPN's free plan offers unlimited data but restricts you to servers in 3 countries and 1 device connection. If you need unlimited data for a single device, ProtonVPN Free wins. If you need multiple devices or more server locations, Windscribe Free is the better choice. Neither free plan reliably unblocks streaming services. **Which country owns Windscribe VPN?** Windscribe Limited is headquartered in Toronto, Ontario, Canada, and the company is privately owned — it has not been acquired by Kape Technologies, Ziff Davis, or any other VPN conglomerate that has bought up competitors. CEO Yegor Sak has remained publicly active with the company since founding it, including personally handling legal disclosures during the 2021 Ukraine incident and the 2025 Greek court case. The Canadian Five Eyes concern is valid, but Windscribe's architecture — RAM-only servers, no logs, distributed authentication — means there is no user data to compel even if a court order were issued. **Is Windscribe shady?** No. This is a common search query driven by skepticism about free VPNs generally, but Windscribe has one of the most transparent track records in the industry. The company self-disclosed the 2021 Ukraine incident rather than waiting for it to leak, published technical post-mortems on Medium and the company blog, commissioned the 2022 Cure53 and 2024 PacketLabs audits, open-sourced their apps on GitHub, and publicly documented the 2025 Greek court dismissal and Dutch server seizure. That is the opposite of shady. The legitimate criticism is the thin audit trail compared to NordVPN or Mullvad — not hidden ownership or undisclosed data practices. ## How Windscribe Compares See how Windscribe measures up in our head-to-head comparisons: - [**Windscribe vs NordVPN**](/compare/windscribe-vs-nordvpn) — How does it compare to the market leader? - [**Windscribe vs Surfshark**](/compare/windscribe-vs-surfshark) — Value comparison with unlimited devices ## Sources & References This review is based on our own testing of Windscribe's Windows, macOS, Android, and browser-extension clients plus primary-source verification via the following references. Screenshots of the homepage, Pro pricing page, and free-plan features page were captured on windscribe.com (April 2026). 1.
